conversation.speaker: doctor
conversation.text: Hello, I'm Doctor Sara. What happened?

conversation.speaker: patient
conversation.text: Hi doctor... I fell down the stairs about an hour ago, and my arm hurts really badly.

conversation.speaker: doctor
conversation.text: I'm sorry to hear that. Which arm is injured?

conversation.speaker: patient
conversation.text: My right arm. I can't move it properly.

conversation.speaker: doctor
conversation.text: On a scale from 1 to 10, how severe is the pain?

conversation.speaker: patient
conversation.text: It's about a 9. It gets worse when I try to move it.

conversation.speaker: doctor
conversation.text: Did you notice any swelling or deformity in the arm?

conversation.speaker: patient
conversation.text: Yes, it's swollen, and it looks a bit bent in the middle.

conversation.speaker: doctor
conversation.text: Alright, try to keep it as still as possible. Did you hear any snapping sound when you fell?

conversation.speaker: patient
conversation.text: Yes, I think I heard a crack when I landed.

conversation.speaker: doctor
conversation.text: Okay. Are you feeling any numbness or tingling in your fingers?

conversation.speaker: patient
conversation.text: A little bit of tingling, yes.

conversation.speaker: doctor
conversation.text: Can you move your fingers at all?

conversation.speaker: patient
conversation.text: Yes, but it hurts when I do.

conversation.speaker: doctor
conversation.text: That's helpful. Based on your symptoms — severe pain, swelling, deformity, and the cracking sound — this is very likely a fracture.

conversation.speaker: patient
conversation.text: A fracture? So it's broken?

conversation.speaker: doctor
conversation.text: Yes, it appears to be a broken arm. We'll confirm with an X-ray immediately.

conversation.speaker: patient
conversation.text: Will I need surgery?

conversation.speaker: doctor
conversation.text: That depends on the type of fracture. If the bones are well aligned, a cast may be enough. If not, we may need to realign them or consider surgery.

conversation.speaker: patient
conversation.text: Okay... it really hurts.

conversation.speaker: doctor
conversation.text: We'll give you pain relief right away and immobilize your arm with a splint to prevent further injury.

conversation.speaker: patient
conversation.text: How long will it take to heal?

conversation.speaker: doctor
conversation.text: Most arm fractures take about 6 to 8 weeks to heal, depending on the severity.

conversation.speaker: patient
conversation.text: Alright. Thank you, doctor.

conversation.speaker: doctor
conversation.text: You're welcome. Let's get you to X-ray now and take care of this properly.
